text.heading: Lack of quality friendly games frustrates Siaisia
text.p: National Under-20 Coach Samson Siasia has expressed frustration at the inability of his boys to play friendly matches which are up to standard in preparation for the Under-20 World Cup which will hold in Egypt in September.
Siasia stated that he was disappointed he will not be playing the Young Pharaohs of Egypt before the World Cup.

He said: "I don’t even know what is going on. But I am very disappointed. I was actually looking forward to playing friendly games with good football teams. For instance that of Malaysia was because of the swine flu .

"But when we go to Qatar and come back we will arrange a game between Ghana or Ivory Coast. We need to play games not just any game at all. Hw do you access these players without playing a good game? So it’s going to be very difficult. We will come back from Qatar and we will try to play some friendly matches before we go to Spain."
The Flying Eagles who won last year's Intercontinental Cup in Malaysia, have continued to experienced hiccups in their various attempts which include the assembling of players.
On June 23 the Flying Eagles will take on the home-based Super Eagles once again in friendly. It could be recalled that the home-based Eagles defeated the Siaisa side by 1-0 few weeks ago.
Meanwhile, the team is expected to go on a recess for ten days after which they are expected to assemble back for a training tour to Qatar on the 4th of July.
Nigeria was drawn in group B alongside Spain, Venezuela and Tahiti in the Under-20 World Cup to be hosted by Egypt.
